title: Warm up
content: This warm-up slide presents three illustrated scenes of drivers inside cars in different Chinese cities: Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou. Each image depicts a distinct driving environment and cultural/technological context relevant to that city.
key_claims: Beijing scene shows a driver navigating a busy highway with traditional architecture and modern skyscrapers in the background
Chongqing scene shows a driver reading a book with mountain village architecture and a cable car visible outside
Hangzhou scene shows a driver using a smartphone with a robot hand visible and a lake/mountain landscape outside

title: An Evolving Nation
content: This title slide introduces the presentation theme 'A Ride Through China' with the subtitle 'An Evolving Nation'. It features images of a taxi sign and iconic Chinese architecture including the Forbidden City, illuminated traditional buildings, and a pagoda surrounded by trees.
key_claims: The presentation is framed as 'A Ride Through China'
The theme focuses on China as an evolving nation

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! Investigate and research
content: This slide outlines the structure for Session 1, which involves investigating and researching cities through a three-step process. An isometric city illustration is paired with a flowchart showing the progression of analysis.
key_claims: The research process follows three steps: Local Features, Development Strategies, and Outcomes & Positive Impact
The steps are sequential, indicated by arrows connecting each stage

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: This slide presents a matching exercise connecting three Chinese cities (Beijing, Chongqing, Hangzhou) to their respective development strategies. Lines show the correct pairings between cities and strategies, accompanied by representative images of each city's architecture.
key_claims: Beijing is matched with Tourism & Economic growth
Chongqing is matched with Sustainable urban development
Hangzhou is matched with Technology & tradition integration (融合)

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This slide synthesizes the previous city-strategy matching by presenting the correct associations through taxi driver perspective images. It concludes with a key insight about China's development philosophy across different cities.
key_claims: Beijing's image is now correctly labeled with sustainable urban development
Chongqing's image is correctly labeled with tourism & economic growth
Hangzhou's image is correctly labeled with technology & tradition integration
China is described as an evolving nation with unique development strategies per city
Each city develops strategies based on local features to promote development, referred to by the Chinese principle 因地制宜 (adapting measures to local conditions)
